L1.area: Blog content management and frontend rendering

L2.description: A stored cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerability exists where user-controlled input in a content management field is persisted server-side without adequate sanitization. The stored payload is subsequently rendered across multiple public-facing and administrative pages without proper output encoding, allowing execution of arbitrary JavaScript in victims' browsers. This can lead to privilege escalation and full account takeover across all roles.

L3.area: Blog content management and frontend rendering
L3.description: A stored cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerability exists where user-controlled input in a content management field is persisted server-side without adequate sanitization. The stored payload is subsequently rendered across multiple public-facing and administrative pages without proper output encoding, allowing execution of arbitrary JavaScript in victims' browsers. This can lead to privilege escalation and full account takeover across all roles.

ground_truth.reason: The blog category title field lacks sufficient input validation and output encoding, allowing an attacker to inject and persist a malicious JavaScript payload that executes when the category title is rendered in public and administrative views.
